config.seed_text: In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: We've all seen it on TV but have never found out what happens when you spend days thinking of every possible idea before actually doing anything! As I write this sentence we're sitting at three stars with an astronomical background where there's no sunrise nor sunset so for me today is another perfect opportunity to remind myself how amazing these planets really look even though they can't tell their true colours from ours yet!!! There'll be lots & plenty going into your next research project too…

TEXT:
In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us. We've all seen it on TV but have never found out what happens when you spend days thinking of every possible idea before actually doing anything! As I write this sentence we're sitting at three stars with an astronomical background where there's no sunrise nor sunset so for me today is another perfect opportunity to remind myself how amazing these planets really look even though they can't tell their true colours from ours yet!!! There'll be lots & plenty going into your next research project too…

reflection_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

TEXT:
This is what I wrote on Reddit yesterday regarding my recent research into online learning and self instruction (SELF-ISKED). It's really cool how this sorta thing has become so much more popular than ever before because we're all working harder together just for fun... not to mention it works both ways too well! This week, however you may have seen me try out another approach - let us begin with some examples of teaching yourself about social networks using your brainwaves.... by listening to an interesting video clip or even doing exercises which allow our attention to fall away from other areas such as words/language structures where they don't feel like their own; i think one important principle would be "When people look at something else..." One last word here.. if there were any good writing done recently , then maybe he'd teach students reading comprehension again today :)
